Here's My Review. | Matthew Setter (Software Engineer and Technical Author, specialising in PHP, Go, and DevOps)","url":"https://matthewsetter.com/asciidoc-plugin-for-intellij-review/"},{"title":"GitHub - 30-reactions/1119-asciidoctor-intellij-plugin: AsciiDoc plugin for products on the IntelliJ platform (IDEA, RubyMine, etc)","url":"https://github.com/30-reactions/1119-asciidoctor-intellij-plugin"},{"title":"asciidoctor-intellij-plugin/doc/users-guide/modules/ROOT/pages/technical-writing/index.adoc at main · asciidoctor/asciidoctor-intellij-plugin","url":"https://github.com/asciidoctor/asciidoctor-intellij-plugin/blob/main/doc/users-guide/modules/ROOT/pages/technical-writing/index.adoc"},{"title":"asciidoctor-intellij-plugin/CHANGELOG.adoc at main · asciidoctor/asciidoctor-intellij-plugin","url":"https://github.com/asciidoctor/asciidoctor-intellij-plugin/blob/main/CHANGELOG.adoc"}]

Based on my search, here's what I found regarding IntelliJ IDEA documentation plugins for AsciiDoc and related formats:

## AsciiDoc Plugin for IntelliJ IDEA

A plugin for the IntelliJ platform (IntelliJ IDEA, RubyMine, etc.) that provides support for the AsciiDoc markup language. It also supports Antora, the documentation CMS build with AsciiDoc.

### Key Features

Edit AsciiDoc files in the IDE with syntax highlighting, auto-completion and a live preview.(no content)The AsciiDoc plugin provides an as-you-type preview, syntax highlighting, auto-completion and content validation in one plugin. The IntelliJ IDE offers a great integration with Git and also plugins for spell and grammar checking.(no content)Recognizes the AsciiDoc file extension (.adoc, .asciidoc, .ad) and provides a two-pane AsciiDoc preview editor based on Asciidoctor with a live preview.

### Additional Capabilities

Convert AsciiDoc to Microsoft Word (DOCX) by using pandoc. Refactor Markdown to AsciiDoc: Right-click on a file, or use the Refactor menu, and the Markdown document will be replaced by the AsciiDoc equivalent. Also, supports undo if the user is not happy with the result!

### 2025 Compatibility

The plugin has compatibility with EAP 2025.1. JavaFX was removed as a preview option as it is no longer supported by JetBrains.

### Installation

You can install the plugin (named "AsciiDoc") from the plugins section inside your JetBrains IDE or download it from the JetBrains Plugin Repository.

### Comparison with reStructuredText

Of all the formatting languages, AsciiDoc's approach to table formatting is noted as "the most accessible, flexible, and accommodating." It has been said to save notable time compared to Markdown or reStructuredText's approach.

---

**Note:** The search results didn't return specific information about RFC-style documentation plugins or dedicated reStructuredText plugins for IntelliJ IDEA in 2025. The main documentation plugin actively maintained and recommended is the AsciiDoc plugin. If you need specific reStructuredText support, you may want to search for that separately.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "RFC format document creation tools 2025 2026 alternatives xml2rfc AsciiDoc"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - metanorma/asciidoctor-rfc: AsciiRFC: an AsciiDoc/asciidoctor backend to produce RFC XML v3 (RFC 7991) and v2 (RFC 7749)","url":"https://github.com/metanorma/asciidoctor-rfc"},{"title":"Mappings Between XML2RFC v3 and AsciiDoc","url":"https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc-02.xml"},{"title":"GitHub - metanorma/rfc-asciidoc-rfc: AsciiRFC: Preparing Internet-Drafts And RFCs In AsciiDoc","url":"https://github.com/metanorma/rfc-asciidoc-rfc"},{"title":"AsciiRFC: Authoring Internet-Drafts And RFCs Using AsciiDoc","url":"https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ribose-asciirfc-08.xml"},{"title":"draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc-06 - Mappings Between XML2RFC v3 and AsciiDoc","url":"https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc/"},{"title":"draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc-05 - Mappings Between XML2RFC v3 and AsciiDoc","url":"https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc/05/"},{"title":"mmark package - github.com/miekg/mmark - Go Packages","url":"https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/miekg/mmark"},{"title":"xml2rfc/CHANGELOG.md at main · ietf-tools/xml2rfc","url":"https://github.com/ietf-tools/xml2rfc/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md"},{"title":"GitHub - ietf-tools/xml2rfc: Generate RFCs and IETF drafts from document source in XML according to the IETF xml2rfc v2 and v3 vocabularies","url":"https://github.com/ietf-tools/xml2rfc"},{"title":"IETF Tools","url":"https://tools.ietf.org/"}]

Here are the search results for RFC format document creation tools and alternatives to xml2rfc and AsciiDoc:

## Main Tools for RFC Document Creation

### xml2rfc (Official IETF Tool)
The IETF uses a specific format for the standards and other documents it publishes as RFCs, and for the draft documents which are produced when developing documents for publications. There exists a number of different tools to facilitate the formatting of drafts and RFCs according to the existing rules, and this tool, xml2rfc, is one of them. It takes as input an xml file that contains the text and meta-information about author names etc., and transforms it into suitably formatted output.(no content)The input xml file should follow the grammars in RFC7749 (for v2 documents) or RFC7991 (for v3 documents). RFCXML vocabulary reference is available at authors.ietf.org.(no content)It also provides conversion from v2 to v3, and can run the preptool on its input. xml2rfc is available as Python package.

### AsciiDoc-Based Alternatives

**AsciiRFC / asciidoctor-rfc (Metanorma)**
asciidoctor-rfc lets you write Internet-Drafts and RFCs in AsciiDoc, the "asciidoctor-way".(no content)While the RFC XML v2 output of this gem is still valid, the RFC XML v3 output of the gem will not work with the updated xml2rfc tool, as this gem does not include the modifications to RFC XML v3 subsequent to RFC 7991. Use metanorma-ietf to generate valid RFC XML v3 output.

**draft-petithuguenin-xml2rfc-asciidoc**
This document specifies a mapping between XML2RFC v3 and AsciiDoc. The goal of this mapping and its associated tooling is to make writing an Internet-Draft as simple as possible, by converting any AsciiDoc formatted document into a valid Internet-Draft, ready to be submitted to the IETF. This is still work in progress and for the time being this mapping only ensures that any valid XML2RFC element can be generated from AsciiDoc.

### Mmark (Markdown-Based Alternative)
Mmark is a fork of blackfriday which is a Markdown processor implemented in Go. It supports a number of extensions, inspired by Leanpub, kramdown and Asciidoc, that allows for large documents to be written.(no content)Currently Html, XML2RFC v3 and XML2RFC v2 implementations are provided.(no content)Mmark adds the following syntax elements to black friday: TOML titleblock. Including other files. More enumerated lists and task-lists. Table and codeblock captions.

### XMLMind XML Editor Add-on
This is a WYSIKN (What You See Is Kinda Neat) addon for the very configurable XMLMind XML Editor ("xxe"). The personal version of xxe is free and very capable. The addon is capable of graphical editing of sections, anchors, lists, cross-references, etc. and allows word processor-like behavior of "enter" to create a new paragraph or list item.

### Docker-Based Toolchains
To use mmark and xml2rfc without installing and configuring the separate software packages and dependencies, you can also use mmark via a Docker container. You can use https://github.com/paulej/rfctools to build a Docker image or you can use the one already created and available in Docker Hub.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the best current alternative for creating readable and simple formatted documents similar to IETF RFCs, preferably in a simple way or within IntelliJ.

Based on my research, I can provide a comprehensive answer covering:

1. **AsciiDoc** - The most practical alternative with excellent IntelliJ support
2. **Mmark** - Markdown-based option for RFC-style docs
3. **xml2rfc** - The official IETF tool (more complex)

Let me also search for more information about markdown-based alternatives and perhaps look at the actual structure comparison.

Web search results for query: "mmark markdown RFC document generator tutorial example 2025"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - mmarkdown/mmark at v2.0.40","url":"https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark/tree/v2.0.40"},{"title":"Mmark: powerful markdown parser","url":"https://mmark.miek.nl/"},{"title":"GitHub - mmarkdown/mmark: Mmark: a powerful markdown processor in Go geared towards the IETF","url":"https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark"},{"title":"Drafting in Markdown | Internet-Draft Author Resources","url":"https://authors.ietf.org/drafting-in-markdown"},{"title":"GitHub - miekg/mmark: **OBSOLETE** See https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark","url":"https://github.com/miekg/mmark"},{"title":"mmark command - github.com/mmarkdown/mmark/v2 - Go Packages","url":"https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/mmarkdown/mmark/v2"},{"title":"mmark package - github.com/miekg/mmark - Go Packages","url":"https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/miekg/mmark"},{"title":"Writing RFCs with Markdown - danielfett.de","url":"https://danielfett.de/2019/02/28/writing-rfcs-with-markdown/"},{"title":"GitHub - martinthomson/mmark: Mmark: a powerful markdown processor in Go geared towards the IETF","url":"https://github.com/martinthomson/mmark"},{"title":"About | Mmark: powerful markdown parser","url":"https://mmark.miek.nl/post/about/"}]

Here are the search results for mmark markdown RFC document generator:

## Overview

Mmark is a powerful markdown processor written in Go, geared towards writing IETF documents. It is also suited for writing complete books and other technical documentation, like the Learning Go book.(no content)It provides an advanced markdown dialect that processes file(s) to produce internet-drafts in XML RFC 7991 format. Mmark can produce xml2rfc (RFC 7991), HTML5 output, and manual pages.

## Getting Started

The easiest way to get started is to download one of the precompiled binaries. And familiarize yourself with markdown and its syntax and read their syntax document.(no content)You can download a binary or optionally build mmark yourself. You'll need a working Go environment, then check out the code. Outputting HTML5 is done with the `-html` switch.

## Example Usage

Some examples are shipped with mmark. Compile to XML and then to HTML with `./mmark -2 main.md > /tmp/xml-2; xml2rfc --html /tmp/xml-2`(no content)The rfc/ directory contains a couple of example RFCs that can be built via the v3 tool chain.

## Creating IETF Documents

To create an Independent IETF Document, use a title block with specific settings where `ipr` and `submissiontype` are the important settings, such as `ipr = "none"` and `submissiontype = "independent"`.

## Docker Support

A container image is automatically built on every release and is available from the GitHub Container Registry. The image can be used with docker run commands.

## Key Features

Mmark adds syntax elements to gomarkdown/markdown including special sections and including other files with the option to specify line ranges, regular expressions and/or prefix each line with a string.(no content)BCP14 (RFC 2119) keyword detection is also supported.

## Resources

- **Main Repository**: https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark
- **Official Documentation**: https://mmark.miek.nl/
- **Go Package**: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/mmarkdown/mmark/v2 (Published: May 26, 2025)

It's recommended to read the mmark syntax manual to learn about the features supported by mmark. If you are interested in starting a new draft using markdown, you can look at example draft repositories as starting points.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive answer. Let me summarize the best options:

1. **AsciiDoc** - Best for IntelliJ integration, readable plain text, widely used
2. **Mmark (Markdown)** - Simpler syntax, IETF-oriented, produces RFC XML
3. **xml2rfc** - Official IETF tool but more complex

I'll present these options with practical recommendations.

content.text:
## Melhores Alternativas para Documentos Estilo RFC

### 1. **AsciiDoc** (Recomendado para IntelliJ)

A melhor opção se você quer integração nativa com IntelliJ:

- **Plugin**: [AsciiDoc Plugin for IntelliJ](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/7391-asciidoc) - preview em tempo real, syntax highlighting, autocompletion
- Sintaxe limpa e legível como texto puro
- Pode gerar RFC XML v3 via [asciidoctor-rfc](https://github.com/metanorma/asciidoctor-rfc)
- Conversão para DOCX, HTML, PDF

```asciidoc
= RFC: Título do Documento
Autor <email@example.com>
v1.0, 2026-02-02
:toc:

== Abstract

Descrição breve do documento.

== Introdução

Lorem ipsum...

=== Subseção

Conteúdo detalhado.
```

### 2. **Mmark** (Markdown para RFCs)

Se prefere Markdown puro e quer output RFC-compliant:

- [Mmark](https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark) - processador Markdown em Go focado em IETF
- Produz XML RFC 7991 diretamente
- Suporte a BCP14 keywords (MUST, SHALL, etc.)
- [Guia oficial IETF para Markdown](https://authors.ietf.org/drafting-in-markdown)

```markdown
%%%
title = "Título do Documento"
abbrev = "doc-titulo"
ipr = "none"
submissiontype = "independent"
%%%

{mainmatter}

# Introdução

Este documento descreve...

# Requisitos

The key words "MUST", "SHOULD" are defined in [@!RFC2119].
```

### 3. **xml2rfc** (Oficial IETF)

- [xml2rfc](https://github.com/ietf-tools/xml2rfc) - ferramenta oficial
- Mais verboso, mas output garantidamente compatível
- Instalação: `pip install xml2rfc`

---

### Recomendação Prática

| Cenário | Ferramenta |
|---------|------------|
| Trabalhar no IntelliJ | **AsciiDoc + Plugin** |
| Sintaxe mais simples possível | **Mmark (Markdown)** |
| RFC real para IETF | **Mmark → xml2rfc** |
| Documentação técnica interna | **AsciiDoc** |

**Para IntelliJ**: Instale o plugin AsciiDoc via `Settings → Plugins → Marketplace → "AsciiDoc"`. Você terá editor split com preview live, navegação por seções, e export direto.

Sources:
- [AsciiDoc IntelliJ Plugin](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/7391-asciidoc)
- [Mmark GitHub](https://github.com/mmarkdown/mmark)
- [IETF Drafting in Markdown](https://authors.ietf.org/drafting-in-markdown)
- [xml2rfc](https://github.com/ietf-tools/xml2rfc)
- [AsciiRFC](https://github.com/metanorma/asciidoctor-rfc)
